page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors built on the ARM64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in memory bandwidth, with read-only and write large-block tests placing in the top 10%. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as demonstrated by top-tier scores in Geekbench multi-core and software compilation tests. Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 10%. Single-core performance and LLM inference prompt processing generally fall into the average range, though text generation with medium-sized models shows strong performance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation deliver high compute density and memory throughput per resource unit. The high network bandwidth of 150 Gbps makes it suitable for network-bound workloads. This instance is optimized for workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded compute tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 96.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and does not include local storage or GPUs. The instance is highly optimized for network performance, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities. Single-core execution and certain LLM inference tasks perform at average levels. Qualitatively, the dedicated ARM architecture provides high resource density and throughput. This instance is best suited for high-throughput web serving, distributed database operations, software compilation, and network-intensive compute workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 96 GB of RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores, high memory bandwidth, and 150 Gbps network throughput for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier performance in software compilation, database operations, and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for network-intensive, multi-threaded compute workloads
